questions.question: A warehouse has a total capacity of 1000 units and currently stores 800 units. What is the storage utilization?
What is the primary benefit of using AGVs in smart warehouses?

explanations.a: AGVs reduce manual labor through automation.
explanations.b: AGVs automate the transportation of goods, reducing cycle times.
explanations.c: Digital twin integration improves inventory visibility.

questions.question: If incoming orders increase significantly while storage capacity remains constant, what is the most likely outcome?

explanations.b: High orders may strain stock availability.
explanations.c: Unloading and storing more items leads to higher congestion risks.
explanations.d: Robot utilization will rise to handle the workload.

questions.question: Which KPI indicates how effectively warehouse robots are being used?

explanations.a: Inventory health measures stock safety levels.
explanations.c: Robot utilization measures the active workload percentage of the AGVs.
explanations.d: Warehouse capacity is the size limit.

questions.question: What happens when inventory levels fall below the inventory threshold?

explanations.b: Rereaching the threshold triggers inventory replenishment to avoid stockouts.
explanations.c: Utilization drops but does not immediately become zero.
explanations.d: Robot utilization may increase to process replenishment.

questions.question: In the simulation, what penalty is applied to the Efficiency Score if AGV Utilization exceeds 80%?

explanations.b: 8% is the penalty for high processing time.
explanations.c: A 12% penalty (P_agv) is applied when AGV utilization exceeds the 80% threshold.

questions.question: What is the baseline multiplier (M_algo) for Processing Time when using the FIFO routing algorithm?

explanations.c: FIFO is the baseline routing algorithm, thus it uses a multiplier of 1.00.
explanations.d: No algorithm increases the base time by 10%.

questions.question: According to the simulation's Congestion Index, what level of congestion is experienced if Storage Utilization is at 88%?

explanations.c: Any storage utilization 85% and above is classified as High congestion.
explanations.d: Critical is not a defined level in this index.

questions.question: How is the number of Pending Orders calculated in the simulation engine?

explanations.a: Pending orders reflect the backlog that cannot be fulfilled by the fleet within the given timeframe.
explanations.b: This formula relates to processing time.
explanations.c: This calculates excess storage, not pending orders.

questions.question: What efficiency bonus (B_algo) is added to the Overall Efficiency Score when using the Balanced Load routing algorithm?

explanations.b: Balanced Load provides a +3% efficiency bonus by reducing congestion delays.
